skill.name: longbridge-watchlist
skill.description: Manage Longbridge watchlists, price alerts, and community stock lists through the Longbridge CLI with explicit confirmation for account-changing actions.
skill.summary: Manage Longbridge watchlists, price alerts, and community stock lists with confirmation before changes.
skill.author: longbridge

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command-execution and blocker patterns. Review found the weak-cryptography and reconnaissance hits are false positives from Markdown text, but the skill intentionally directs agents to run Longbridge CLI commands that can change watchlists, alerts, and stock lists. Publication is acceptable with a warning because the skill includes explicit preview and confirmation controls, and no malicious exfiltration or prompt-injection text was found.

medium_findings.title: Account-changing Longbridge CLI commands
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill instructs agents to run Longbridge CLI commands for creating, renaming, deleting, adding, and removing watchlist, alert, and sharelist data. This is legitimate for the skill, but it can persistently modify a user account and requires strict confirmation handling.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files explicitly document mutating Longbridge commands and require agents to execute them after confirmation. The risk is clear, but the commands are first-party Longbridge operations rather than evidence of malware.

medium_findings.title: Shell command construction from user-supplied values
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill asks agents to construct CLI commands using user-supplied group names, symbols, prices, alert ids, and list ids. The documentation shows quoting for some names and confirmation gates, but agents must still avoid shell injection when passing user values to a terminal.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command templates embed placeholders that come from user requests. No direct vulnerable implementation exists in this Markdown-only skill, so the issue is an operational risk rather than confirmed exploitable code.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded GitHub installation URL
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE but low risk: The skill references the public longbridge-terminal GitHub URL when the CLI is missing. This is user-facing installation guidance, not data exfiltration or a hidden network callback.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is visible in error-handling text and points to the Longbridge terminal project. I found no evidence that the skill sends user data to this URL.

low_findings.title: Weak-cryptography alerts are false positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Static weak-cryptography hits occur on Markdown prose and metadata lines, not cryptographic code. No hashing, cipher selection, credential handling, or cryptographic implementation appears in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review shows ordinary Markdown instructions and no cryptographic APIs or algorithms. The scanner appears to have matched short substrings in natural language.

low_findings.title: System-reconnaissance alerts are false positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged lines describe normal CLI help, authentication, and error-handling flows. I found no instruction to enumerate host files, environment variables, processes, network interfaces, or other system inventory.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations only reference Longbridge CLI usage and help output. There is no semantic evidence of host reconnaissance intent.

dangerous_patterns.title: External CLI execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill relies on external Longbridge CLI commands, including commands that mutate account state after confirmation. This pattern requires careful argument handling and user consent.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: External command usage is central to the documented workflow across all referenced files. The impact is bounded to Longbridge operations but still changes persistent account state.

content.value_statement: Investors need a controlled way to update Longbridge watchlists, alerts, and community stock lists. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Longbridge CLI workflows with confirmation before changes.
actual_capabilities: List Longbridge watchlist groups and symbols using the Longbridge CLI.
Create, rename, delete, add to, and remove from watchlist groups after confirmation.
List, add, enable, disable, and delete Longbridge price alerts after confirmation.
Browse popular community sharelists and inspect sharelist constituents.
Create and manage Longbridge sharelists with a preview and confirmation workflow.
Respond in Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese, or English based on the user request.
limitations: Requires the Longbridge CLI or an available Longbridge MCP fallback.
Mutating operations require Longbridge login and may require trade scope.
Does not place trades or provide independent investment advice.
Command flags can change, so the skill asks agents to check CLI help when unsure.

use_cases.title: Maintain a personal watchlist
use_cases.description: Add, remove, rename, or delete Longbridge watchlist groups with confirmation before any persistent change.

use_cases.title: Set price alert workflows
use_cases.description: Create and manage price alerts for specific symbols, prices, and directions using Longbridge account data.

use_cases.title: Curate community stock lists
use_cases.description: Browse public sharelists, inspect constituents, and manage owned lists for a Longbridge audience.

prompt_templates.title: Show My Watchlists
prompt_templates.prompt: Show my Longbridge watchlist groups and symbols in a concise table.

prompt_templates.title: Add Symbols to a Group
prompt_templates.prompt: Add NVDA.US and AAPL.US to my Longbridge technology watchlist. Preview the change first and wait for my confirmation.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Price Alert
prompt_templates.scenario: Alert setup with missing details checked
prompt_templates.prompt: Set a Longbridge price alert for TSLA.US at 250 USD when the price falls below that level. Ask me to confirm before running it.

prompt_templates.title: Review and Manage Sharelists
prompt_templates.prompt: Find popular Longbridge sharelists, summarize the top candidates, then help me create a new list from selected symbols after confirmation.

output_examples.output: A table of group names, group ids, symbol counts, and symbols, followed by Longbridge as the data source.

output_examples.input: Add MSFT.US to my AI group.
output_examples.output: A preview stating the target group and symbol, then a confirmation question before any command is executed.

output_examples.input: Create a sharelist with TSLA.US and AAPL.US.
output_examples.output: A short plan naming the new list and symbols, a request for explicit confirmation, then a success summary after execution.

best_practices: Preview every create, update, delete, add, remove, enable, disable, or sort action before execution.
Use exact Longbridge symbols and verify group ids, alert ids, and list ids before mutating data.
Run the relevant Longbridge help command when flag names or argument order are uncertain.
anti_patterns: Do not execute mutating commands in the same turn as the initial request.
Do not bypass built-in CLI confirmation prompts or pipe automatic yes responses.
Do not invent prices, directions, ids, symbols, or investment recommendations.

faq.question: Does this skill place trades?
faq.answer: No. It manages watchlists, price alerts, and community stock lists. It does not place buy or sell orders.

faq.question: Does it require a Longbridge account?
faq.answer: Read-only sharelist browsing may not require login. Watchlist and alert changes require a Longbridge login.

faq.question: Why does it ask for confirmation?
faq.answer: Several operations change persistent account data. The skill requires a preview and explicit confirmation before running those commands.

faq.question: Can it work without the Longbridge CLI?
faq.answer: The skill is designed for the Longbridge CLI. It may use an MCP fallback only when available and after the same confirmation checks.

faq.question: Which languages does it support?
faq.answer: It can respond in Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese, or English based on the user request.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk warning. It uses external commands and can modify account data, but no malicious behavior was found.
